organism.name: Haloferax volcanii (strain ATCC 29605 / DSM 3757 / JCM 8879 / NBRC 14742 / NCIMB 2012 / VKM B-1768 / DS2)
Halobacterium volcanii

citation.name: PLoS ONE
citation.title: The complete genome sequence of Haloferax volcanii DS2, a model archaeon.

source.strain: ATCC 29605 / DSM 3757 / JCM 8879 / NBRC 14742 / NCIMB 2012 / VKM B-1768 / DS2

citation.name: PLoS Genet.
citation.title: Phylogenetically driven sequencing of extremely halophilic archaea reveals strategies for static and dynamic osmo-response.

source.strain: ATCC 29605 / DSM 3757 / JCM 8879 / NBRC 14742 / NCIMB 2012 / VKM B-1768 / DS2

citation.name: FEBS Lett.
citation.title: The oxidative pentose phosphate pathway in the haloarchaeon Haloferax volcanii involves a novel type of glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase -- The archaeal Zwischenferment.

comment.text: Catalyzes the oxidative decarboxylation of 6-phosphogluconate to ribulose 5-phosphate and CO(2), with concomitant reduction of NAD to NADH.

kinetics.Vmax: 10.0 umol/min/mg enzyme (with NAD(+) as electron acceptor)
0.42 umol/min/mg enzyme (with NADP(+) as electron acceptor)
kinetics.text: The catalytic efficiency with NAD(+) is about 2000-fold higher than with NADP(+), indicating that NAD(+) is the physiological electron acceptor.

comment.text: Carbohydrate degradation; pentose phosphate pathway.
Homotetramer.
Deletion mutants do not grow on glucose.
Belongs to the 6-phosphogluconate dehydrogenase family.

citation.name: Microbiology
citation.title: Genome of Methanoregula boonei 6A8 reveals adaptations to oligotrophic peatland environments.

comment.text: Component of the A-type ATP synthase that produces ATP from ADP in the presence of a proton gradient across the membrane. The A chain is the catalytic subunit.

reaction.text: ATP + H2O + 4 H(+)(in) = ADP + phosphate + 5 H(+)(out)

comment.text: Has multiple subunits with at least A(3), B(3), C, D, E, F, H, I and proteolipid K(x).
Belongs to the ATPase alpha/beta chains family.

citation.title: Amino acid sequence of the alpha and beta subunits of Methanosarcina barkeri ATPase deduced from cloned genes. Similarity to subunits of eukaryotic vacuolar and F0F1-ATPases.

comment.text: Produces ATP from ADP in the presence of a proton gradient across the membrane. The archaeal alpha chain is a catalytic subunit.

organism.name: Methanocaldococcus jannaschii (strain ATCC 43067 / DSM 2661 / JAL-1 / JCM 10045 / NBRC 100440)
Methanococcus jannaschii

citation.name: Science
citation.title: Complete genome sequence of the methanogenic archaeon, Methanococcus jannaschii.

citation.name: Extremophiles
citation.title: Isolation of a complete A1AO ATP synthase comprising nine subunits from the hyperthermophile Methanococcus jannaschii.

citation.name: J. Biol. Chem.
citation.title: Structure and subunit arrangement of the A-type ATP synthase complex from the archaeon Methanococcus jannaschii visualized by electron microscopy.

reference.scope: STRUCTURE BY ELECTRON MICROSCOPY (18 ANGSTROMS) OF A-TYPE ATP SYNTHASE

citation.name: Biochemistry
citation.title: Small-angle X-ray scattering reveals the solution structure of the peripheral stalk subunit H of the A1AO ATP synthase from Methanocaldococcus jannaschii and its binding to the catalytic A subunit.

comment.text: Component of the A-type ATP synthase that produces ATP from ADP in the presence of a proton gradient across the membrane. The A chain is the catalytic subunit (By similarity). Hydrolyzes ATP, GTP (86% of ATPase rate) and UTP (54% of ATPase rate), has very poor activity on CTP (PubMed:12768457).

reaction.text: ATP + H2O + 4 H(+)(in) = ADP + phosphate + 5 H(+)(out)

comment.text: ATP hydrolysis is inhibited by N',N'-dicyclohexylcarbodiimide.

phDependence.text: Optimum pH is 6.0 for ATP hydrolysis.

comment.text: The N-terminus (approximately residues 106-122) interacts with subunit H (PubMed:17263559). Has multiple subunits with at least A(3), B(3), C, D, E(1 or 2), F, H(2), I and proteolipid K(x) (PubMed:12768457, PubMed:15220347).

Purified ATP synthase is 25.9 nm long. The hydrophilic A1 domain is 9.4 X 11.5 nm, the central stalk is 8.0 X 3.9 nm and the membrane-bound A0 domain is 6.4 X 10.6 nm; the domains are connected by two stalks. ATP is synthesized or hydrolyzed by the A1 domain while ion translocation occurs via the A0 domain.
Belongs to the ATPase alpha/beta chains family.
Extended N-terminus.

property.value: C:proton-transporting two-sector ATPase complex, catalytic domain
F:proton-transporting ATP synthase activity, rotational mechanism
P:proton motive force-driven plasma membrane ATP synthesis
V-type proton ATPase catalytic subunit A
V-type proton ATPase catalytic subunit A
V-type proton ATPase catalytic subunit A
Bovine Mitochondrial F1-atpase, Atp Synthase Beta Chain, Chain D, domain 3
V-TYPE PROTON ATPASE CATALYTIC SUBUNIT A
C-terminal domain of alpha and beta subunits of F1 ATP synthase
